GamePreview.title: Describe the card. Avoid the taboo words.
GamePreview.description: Kuttabu is a modern word game that brings Turkic history and culture into your game nights.

items.title: Built around Turkic history
items.body: A unique card library featuring states, battles, rulers, written works and cultural concepts.

items.title: One device
items.body: Choose the teams and start playing. No rooms, accounts or complicated setup.

items.title: Difficulty levels
items.body: Easy, medium and hard cards create the right pace for every group.

items.title: Kut cards
items.body: Special cards change the rhythm of the game and create a more dynamic experience.

Footer.brandDescription: The Taboo Game of the Turkic World.
Footer.seoText: Kuttabu is a modern Taboo-style word game inspired by Turkic history and culture. The Göktürks, the Ottoman Empire, the Seljuk Empire, the Orkhon Inscriptions, the Battle of Manzikert and hundreds of other historical subjects become an entertaining game experience through carefully prepared cards.
Footer.tagline: The Taboo Game of the Turkic World

sections.body: Kuttabu is a modern Taboo-style word game created to make Turkic history and culture entertaining in social settings. Our aim is not simply to build a mobile game, but to transform a shared cultural heritage into an experience people can enjoy together.

sections.heading: Our Vision
sections.body: To become one of the most comprehensive digital entertainment and knowledge platforms representing the Turkic world by building a strong bridge between cultural games and modern mobile experiences.

sections.heading: Our Mission
sections.body: To offer an accessible, high-quality game experience that supports learning through play and continuously expands through new cards and categories.

sections.heading: The Story of Kuttabu
sections.body: Kuttabu began with the need for a modern, clean and well-designed game that people interested in Turkic history could enjoy with friends.
Today, Kuttabu is more than a game. It is a long-term project designed to introduce Turkic history to new generations through a fresh and social experience.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For feedback, suggestions and partnerships, contact us at support@kuttabu.com.
At Kuttabu, we value the privacy of our users. This policy explains what information may be collected while using our application and website and how that information may be used.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
sections.body: Kuttabu does not require an account for its core game experience. Device information, application version, crash reports and anonymous usage statistics may be collected to improve the product.

sections.heading: How Information Is Used
sections.body: Collected information is used only to improve performance, resolve technical issues, develop new features and enhance the user experience.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
sections.body: Kuttabu may use third-party analytics, crash reporting or store services. These services are governed by their own privacy policies.

sections.heading: Security
sections.body: We use current security practices to protect user information. However, no internet-based method of transmission can be guaranteed to be completely secure.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For questions about this privacy policy, contact support@kuttabu.com.
These terms apply to visitors and users of the Kuttabu mobile application and kuttabu.com. By using Kuttabu, you confirm that you have read, understood and accepted these terms.

sections.heading: Purpose of the Service
sections.body: Kuttabu is a modern Taboo-style game built around Turkic history, culture, states, battles, rulers, written works and historical concepts.

sections.heading: Use of Content
sections.body: Cards, words, prohibited words, written content, visual design, the logo, interface elements and all other original materials belong to Kuttabu and may not be copied, reproduced or used commercially without permission.

sections.heading: Updates
sections.body: Kuttabu reserves the right to update, modify or remove cards, categories, rules, interfaces and features in order to improve the game experience.

sections.heading: User Responsibilities
sections.body: Users agree not to use the application in violation of applicable law, public standards or the rights of third parties.

sections.heading: Disclaimer
sections.body: Historical and cultural content is prepared with care, but Kuttabu is not an academic publication or an official historical authority.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
sections.body: The Kuttabu name, logo, visual identity, software, interface design and original game content are protected by applicable intellectual property laws.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For questions about these terms, contact support@kuttabu.com.
Kuttabu values the privacy and security of personal data. This notice explains how data may be processed, protected and managed in accordance with applicable data protection principles.

sections.heading: Data That May Be Processed
sections.body: The application does not require membership for core use. Device model, operating system version, application version, anonymous crash records and performance information may be processed for security and product improvement.

sections.heading: Purposes of Processing
sections.body: Information is used to improve service quality, analyze performance, identify technical problems, enhance the user experience and protect the application.

sections.heading: Data Retention
sections.body: Personal data is retained only for as long as necessary. When legal and operational requirements end, data is deleted, anonymized or otherwise disposed of appropriately.

sections.heading: User Rights
sections.body: Users may request information about whether their personal data is processed, request correction or deletion, and exercise other rights available under applicable data protection legislation.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Data protection requests can be sent to support@kuttabu.com.
Kuttabu is a modern Taboo-style game built around Turkic history and culture. Players take turns describing the word shown on the screen without using the prohibited words listed on the card.

sections.heading: 1. Create the Teams
sections.body: Create two teams before the game begins. Choose team names and decide which team will start.

sections.heading: 2. Choose the Game Settings
sections.body: Set the round duration, number of rounds, pass limit and taboo penalty before starting the game.

sections.heading: 3. Describe the Word
sections.body: When the timer starts, the narrator tries to help teammates guess the main word without saying any of the prohibited words.

sections.heading: 4. Correct, Pass or Taboo
sections.body: Select Correct when the word is guessed, Pass when you want to skip the card, and Taboo when a prohibited word is used.

sections.heading: 5. End of the Round
sections.body: When the timer ends, play passes to the other team. The team with the highest score after all rounds wins.

sections.heading: Tip
sections.body: Short, clear and creative clues help your team guess more cards while avoiding the prohibited words.
Kuttabu is a team-based word game inspired by Turkic history and culture. The goal is to describe the main word without using the prohibited words and earn the highest score.

sections.heading: Core Rule
sections.body: The narrator describes the main word to teammates but may not use any of the prohibited words shown on the card.

sections.heading: Correct Answer
sections.body: When the team guesses the main word correctly, the card is marked as correct and the team earns a point.

sections.heading: Taboo
sections.body: If the narrator uses one of the prohibited words or directly says the main word, the card is marked as taboo.

sections.heading: Pass
sections.body: The narrator may skip a difficult card. The number of available passes is selected during game setup.

sections.heading: Scoring
sections.body: Under the default scoring system, a correct answer gives +1 point and a taboo gives -1 point.

sections.heading: Time and Rounds
sections.body: Each team describes cards during the selected time limit. After all rounds are completed, the team with the highest score wins.

sections.heading: Winning Team
sections.body: The team with the highest final score wins. If the scores are tied, an additional round may be played.
Kuttabu cards are organized into categories that explore different parts of Turkic history and culture.

sections.heading: Turkic States
sections.body: The Göktürks, Huns, Uyghurs, Seljuks, Ottomans and other Turkic states from different historical periods.

sections.heading: Battles
sections.body: Manzikert, the Conquest of Constantinople, Mohács, Dandanaqan and other major military events.

sections.heading: Rulers
sections.body: Khagans, sultans, padishahs, commanders and leaders who shaped Turkic history.

sections.heading: Written Works
sections.body: The Orkhon Inscriptions, Kutadgu Bilig, Dīwān Lughāt al-Turk and other important written sources.

sections.heading: Epics and Mythology
sections.body: Oghuz Khagan, Ergenekon, the Grey Wolf and major themes from Turkic mythology.

sections.heading: Culture and Concepts
sections.body: Kut, töre, toy, kurultai, oba, khaganate and other concepts reflecting Turkic culture.

sections.heading: How Are Categories Prepared?
sections.body: Each card is designed to create a balanced relationship between the main word and its prohibited words.

sections.heading: Continuously Growing Content
sections.body: The Kuttabu card library continues to expand through new categories, new words and player feedback.
Kuttabu is not a one-time release. It is a continuously developed content project.

sections.heading: Content Updates
sections.body: New cards, states, battles, rulers, periods and cultural concepts are regularly added to the card library.

sections.heading: Game Experience Updates
sections.body: The game flow, card transitions, sounds, vibration and interface details may be improved based on player feedback.

sections.heading: Incorrect Card Corrections
sections.body: Cards reported as inaccurate or unbalanced are reviewed and updated when necessary.

sections.heading: Release Notes
sections.body: Major updates, new features and important changes are published on the release notes page.
The Kuttabu Blog offers clear, accessible articles about Turkic history and culture connected to the game's card library.

sections.heading: Who Were the Göktürks?
sections.body: An introduction to the place of the Göktürks in history and the legacy they left behind.

sections.heading: Turkic States
sections.body: A broad overview of the heritage of Turkic states from the Huns to the Ottoman Empire.

sections.heading: The Seljuk Empire
sections.body: A short introduction to the political and cultural power that transformed Anatolian history.

sections.heading: Ottoman History
sections.body: The major periods of Ottoman history from principality to empire.
Important developments, new features, content packs and release information related to Kuttabu are shared on this page.

sections.heading: Kuttabu Is Preparing for Its First Release
sections.body: The first version will include the core game flow, team settings, card system and a library inspired by Turkic history.
Kuttabu release notes provide a record of major changes, new features, card updates and bug fixes.

sections.heading: v1.0.0 — Initial Release
sections.body: Team-based gameplay, the card description system, correct, pass and taboo actions, a Turkic history card library, settings, profile, results and feedback screens.
Accuracy, balance and playability are important to us. You can report historical errors, incorrect categories or problematic prohibited words.

sections.heading: What Can Be Reported?
sections.body: Incorrect information, overly easy or difficult prohibited words, repeated cards, spelling errors and category mismatches.

sections.heading: How to Report
sections.body: Use the feedback area in the application or email the card name and a short explanation to support@kuttabu.com.

sections.heading: Review Process
sections.body: Reports are reviewed and cards may be corrected, updated or removed from the card library.
Turkic states have left political and cultural legacies across a vast geography stretching from the Central Asian steppes to Anatolia, the Balkans and the Caucasus.

sections.heading: Turkic States in Kuttabu
sections.body: The Göktürks, Huns, Uyghurs, Seljuks, Ottomans and other Turkic states appear through relationships between main words and prohibited words.

sections.heading: Why Are They Important?
sections.body: Turkic states left important legacies in military organization, government, culture, language, art, law and diplomacy.

sections.heading: Use in the Game
sections.body: A card may use Göktürks as the main word and Orkhon, Bilge Khagan, Khagan, Grey Wolf or Inscription as prohibited words.
The Göktürks hold a special place in Turkic history as one of the first political entities to use the name Turk as a state name.

sections.heading: Their Place in History
sections.body: The Göktürks were one of the most important state formations of the steppe world, known for their khaganate system, military organization and wide regional influence.

sections.heading: The Orkhon Inscriptions
sections.body: The inscriptions dedicated to Bilge Khagan, Kül Tigin and Tonyukuk are among the most valuable sources for the Turkic language and historical consciousness.

sections.heading: Göktürk Cards in Kuttabu
sections.body: A Göktürk card may be associated with Orkhon, Bilge Khagan, inscriptions, the Grey Wolf, Khagan, Tonyukuk and Kül Tigin.

sections.heading: Why Is It Fun in the Game?
sections.body: The Göktürks create strong historical associations, but carefully selected prohibited words make the card challenging and entertaining.
The Seljuk Empire played a major role in the rise of Turkic political, military and cultural power in the Islamic world.

sections.heading: Historical Importance
sections.body: The Seljuks influenced a vast geography through their government system, military successes, architecture and scholarly institutions.

sections.heading: Manzikert and Anatolia
sections.body: The Battle of Manzikert opened a new historical period in which Anatolia became a major center of Turkic history.

sections.heading: Seljuk Cards in Kuttabu
sections.body: The Seljuk Empire may be associated with Alp Arslan, Manzikert, Nizam al-Mulk, Anatolia, Sultan, madrasas and caravanserais.

sections.heading: Difficulty in the Game
sections.body: Seljuk cards provide strong associations but carefully chosen prohibited words force narrators to create new connections.
Ottoman history is a long and complex process that stretches from a small principality to an empire spanning three continents.

sections.heading: From Principality to Empire
sections.body: The Ottoman principality gradually became a major empire influential across the Balkans, Anatolia, the Middle East and North Africa.

sections.heading: The Ottoman Empire in Kuttabu
sections.body: Ottoman history appears through rulers, battles, treaties, institutions, architecture and cultural concepts.

sections.heading: Example Card Associations
sections.body: Mehmed the Conqueror, the Conquest of Constantinople, Suleiman the Magnificent, Janissaries, Topkapı Palace, the Imperial Council and the Tulip Era.

sections.heading: Why Is It Strong in the Game?
sections.body: Ottoman history offers a broad vocabulary that makes it suitable for both easy and difficult cards.
The Ottoman Empire grew from a small principality into a major political and cultural power spanning three continents and shaping world history for centuries.

sections.heading: From Principality to Empire
sections.body: The Ottoman principality gradually became a major empire influential across Anatolia, the Balkans, the Middle East and North Africa.

sections.heading: The Ottoman Empire in Kuttabu
sections.body: The Ottoman Empire appears in Kuttabu cards through rulers, battles, treaties, institutions, architecture and cultural concepts.

sections.heading: Example Card Associations
sections.body: Mehmed the Conqueror, the Conquest of Constantinople, Suleiman the Magnificent, Janissaries, Topkapı Palace, the Imperial Council, Enderun and the Tulip Era are strong associations.

sections.heading: Why Is It Strong in the Game?
sections.body: Ottoman history offers a broad vocabulary that makes it suitable for both easy and difficult cards.
